title: Anatta
definition: The Buddhist concept that there is no permanent self or soul.

difficulty_levels.beginner: The idea that 'you' are not a separate, permanent entity, but rather a temporary collection of thoughts, feelings, and sensations.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The Buddhist teaching that the self is an illusion, composed of impermanent mental and physical processes, which leads to the cessation of suffering through enlightenment.
difficulty_levels.expert: A philosophical concept rooted in Buddhism that challenges the notion of a permanent, unchanging self. It posits that 'you' are an ever-changing collection of thoughts, feelings, and sensations without a central controller or ego, leading to spiritual liberation when this understanding is fully realized.
